title: Grammar Level 3: Grammatical Gender
description: Every noun in Hindi has a grammatical gender (Masculine or Feminine). Adjectives must change their endings to agree with the noun's gender.
keyPoints: Masculine nouns often end in -ā (e.g., लड़का - boy). Adjectives end in -ā (बड़ा लड़का).
Feminine nouns often end in -ī (e.g., लड़की - girl). Adjectives end in -ī (बड़ी लड़की).

title: Adjective Agreement
explanation: To describe a masculine noun like 'कमरा' (room), the adjective 'बड़ा' (big) ends in -ā. For a feminine noun like 'किताब' (book), it changes to 'बड़ी'.
simpleRule: Match the adjective suffix (-ā for masculine, -ī for feminine) to the noun's gender.

prompt_vi: Chọn sự hòa hợp tính từ đúng

title: Gender Agreement Mastery
takeaways: Modified adjectives based on masculine (-ā) or feminine (-ī) endings.
